pi_items.source: CAMEO Chemicals database (https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/chemical/2577) and safety assessment request file
CAMEO Chemicals database and safety assessment request file
CAMEO Chemicals reports boiling point as 176.2°F at 760 mmHg, which converts to 80.1°C: (176.2 - 32) × 5/9 = 80.1
CAMEO Chemicals reports flash point as 12°F, which converts to -11.1°C: (12 - 32) × 5/9 = -11.1
CAMEO Chemicals reports specific gravity as 0.879 at 68°F
Safety assessment request file and setup_task.sh script confirm output location

privileged_info_summary: All metadata claims have been independently verified against CAMEO Chemicals (NOAA's publicly available chemical database at https://cameochemicals.noaa.gov/). The task requires an agent to search for benzene (CAS 71-43-2) on CAMEO Chemicals and extract physical properties. Verified data: Benzene has a boiling point of 80.1°C (176.2°F), flash point of -11°C (12°F), specific gravity of 0.879 at 68°F, melting point of 5.5°C (41.9°F), vapor pressure of 76 mmHg at 68°F, LEL of 1.4%, UEL of 8%, IDLH of 500 ppm, vapor density of 2.77 relative to air, and water solubility of 1-5 mg/mL. The data is sourced from a real, public NOAA service and is not synthetic.
